acceptanceCriteria.criterion: One recovery path owns each local workspace
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: Per-workspace global transactions serialize local creation, quarantine and atomic publication.
A ready marker bound to path and inode is required; incomplete directories are quarantined before bootstrap.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Partial and root-owned artifacts cannot leave the canonical path half-removed
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: Bootstrap occurs in a staging sibling and is atomically renamed only after success; original data remains under quarantine.
Current-main dirty, unreadable and unpublished workspace preservation was retained during conflict integration.
Parent implementation passed 332 tests, 100% coverage, Credo and Dialyzer; final exact HEAD passed fmt-check and all 60 affected recovery tests.

summary: Workspace recovery is serialized, quarantines invalid state, bootstraps off-path and publishes atomically.
